Reggie Franklin,[2]better known asA-Train, is a major character and former antagonist of the Amazon seriesThe Boys. He also appears as a minor character in its spin-off seriesGen V. He was a speedster who used to be a part of the team known as theTeenage Kix, and was a former member ofThe Seven. He inadvertently killedRobin Ward, girlfriend ofHughie Campbell, with his powers while high onCompound V. This action led to Hughie becoming his archenemy, ultimately leading him to joinThe Boys. A-train was a complex individual with many inner struggles. Initially, like most "superheroes," A-Train was greedy, self-centered, egotistical, and mostly interested in fame, fortune, and publicity rather than helping others. In fact, he displayed a remarkable level of cutthroat ruthlessness, possibly even to the point of sociopathy. It is also implied during a conversation with Starlight about The Seven possibly losing their financial assets that A-Train's obsession with maintaining his wealth and celebrity status might have been rooted in his impoverished upbringing and measures his own self-worth by his lavish lifestyle of being a Vought-owned supe. Initially, Reggie desired to do good in the world, but under Vought’s influence and unimaginable pressure, this would corrupt him to his very core at the cost of his own integrity and self-preservation. In his first appearance, he was shown to be extremely careless. He killedRobin Wardby running through her, albeit accidentally while on Compound V. Further, there were hints throughout the series that he had caused this kind of incident before. Not only did he show no remorse over an accident that should have been traumatizing, but he displayed incredible callousness when he later joked about this incident to a fellow "supe," laughing at Robin's death. Although he personally apologized to Hughie for killing Robin, he did this only because he was compelled to by Vought as a concession for Hughie Campbell

[The] A-Trainis a major antagonist of the superhero-drama comic seriesThe Boys. He was a speedster who became a member of the Seven to replace theirprevious speedster. He is the archenemy ofHughie Campbell. He inadvertently killed Hughie Campbell's girlfriend, while chasing a criminal. The one event kick-started the events in the series, by creating a vengeance against superheroes in Hughie, and Hughie's involvement inthe Boys. Like most "heroes", A-Train is self-centered and mostly interested in fame, fortune, and publicity rather than helping others. In fact, he displays a remarkable lack of empathy, possibly even to the point of sociopathy. He is a "superhero" with super-speed whose carelessness was responsible for the death of Robin, Wee Hughie's girlfriend, in the first issue, accidentally tossing over her a super villain he was chasing and crushing her into a brick wall. Initially he feels genuinely bad about it, but it's not long after that he just brushes it off as nothing. A-Train is the crudest member of the Seven, being the one who most openly enjoys humiliating Starlight. A heavy drug user and bitter over Starlight, A-Train openly expresses a desire to sexually assault her again. One such attempt results in him being temporarily blinded in one eye from her. Due of his immaturity, A-Train is not respected by the rest of The Seven, with his only real friend beingJack from Jupiter, whom A-Train later sides against when Jack's reputation is ruined. A-Train has also shown an immature side. When Starlight spoke at The Seven's meeting table for the first time, A-Train made multiple childish noises that sounded similar to some sort of slurping, earning the ire of Homelander due to it being in front ofJames Stillwell, and leading him to remind him that he isn't inTeenage Kixanymore and that there are times when he has to be serious. When he was captured by Billy Butcher and at the mercy of Hughie, he begs and sobs for his life in a manner of a crying toddler.

characters.description: Alastair AdanaActorGoran VisnjicBilly Zane(reenactment in "Not Without My Dolphin")GenderMaleSpeciesHumanHair ColorBlackAgeMid to Late 40sOccupationChairman of the Church of the CollectiveFamilyUnnamed GrandfatherEthnicityCaucasian-AmericanStatusDeceasedThe BoysFirst AppearanceLast Appearance"We Gotta Go Now""What I Know"FateDeath Episode"What I Know"Alastair’s head is exploded by Victoria Neuman. Alastair Adanais a supporting antagonist inSeason Twoof the Amazon seriesThe Boys.He was the chairman of theChurch of the Collective. It was revealed byStan Edgarthat Alastair's grandfather was the original founder and leader of theChurch of the Collectivewhen it was "just a start up", confirming that Alastair has had ties to the Church ever since he was born and that he had likely inherited the position as leader from his father and grandfather. Alastair Adana is quick with a smile and acts benevolent, promising disgraced celebrities to help regain their reputation, but it's a thin veneer for the manipulative, power-hungry cult leader he is. The moment people stop dancing to his tune, he resorts to blackmailing them or releasing private and sensitive information about them. He's not lacking in confidence, thanks to having enough influence to getStan Edgarto sit at his table to discuss matters. He takes it further with his offer toVictoria Neumanabout bringingVoughtdown with intel onSupesthat he possesses, if she expedites theChurch of the Collective's tax-exempt status. This ultimately becomes his downfall, leading to his death at Neuman's hands. He really loves Fresca, which he seems to force on the rest of his church members, and looks quite offended when Edgar andThe Deepexpress their disliking for the drink.

characters.name: Alastair Adana's Grandfather
characters.description: Alastair Adana's Grandfatheris the founder of theChurch of the Collectiveand the grandfather ofAlastair Adana. During the1950s,Alastair Adana's grandfather established theChurch of the Collectiveas a self-help group. Despite its humble beginnings, the movement quickly gained a large following, attracting numerous prominent figures and celebrities who embraced its "New Science of Self-Renewal" as a bold exploration of the human mind. In time, Alastair succeeded his grandfather as leader of the Church and continued to expand its influence and membership.

characters.name: Andre Anderson
characters.description: Andre AndersonActorChance PerdomoAliasesGuardian of Godolkin(by public, formerly)GenderMaleSpeciesSupeHair ColorBlackEye ColorBrownAgeLate Teens to Early 20sOccupationFormer Student atGodolkin UniversityFormer Member of theGuardians of GodolkinPatient atElmira Adult Rehabilitation CenterFamilyMr. Anderson- FatherLaila Anderson- Mother †EthnicityAfrican-AmericanStatusDeceasedThe BoysOnly Appearance"Wisdom of the Ages"(Photograph)Gen VFirst AppearanceLast Appearance"God U.""Guardians of Godolkin"FateDied from a ruptured aneurysm from overexerting himself while trying to use his powers to escape by bending a reinforced steel door after being sent to Elmira in Cate & Sam's place, later revealed to have been a failed Cipher experiment.Image GalleryNotable Quotes Andre Andersonis the deuteragonist ofSeason Oneand the posthumous overarching protagonist ofSeason Twoof the Amazon seriesGen V, a spin-off series ofThe Boys. He was a junior atGodolkin Universityand the son ofPolarity. Andre was born toPolarityandLailain the early 2000s, and shortly after his birth, his parents acceptedVought's offer to inject him withCompound V, subsequently transforming him into aSupeand granting him superhuman powers and abilities. Andre grew up as an only child and experienced a positive childhood. As a result, he formed a close bond with his father, watching hisArea 51movie tetralogy numerous times and attempting to copy his heroic stance and quote.[1]Sometime later, Laila passed away, which devastated Andre and his father. However, both of them pushed through this traumatic event and eventually overcame her death.[2] Andre is a complicated character; while he wants to be a hero, he is unsure of succeeding his father and becoming the new Polarity although he doesn't want to let him down. He also betraysGolden Boy, his best friend, by hooking up with Cate, his girlfriend, despite feeling guilty he does it because he loves her. After Luke's death, Andre begins to feel depressed since he was never truly honest with him and thought he never knew about him and Cate, he also feels very stressed with becoming the new top ranked student, feeling he is not suit enough for it and that he's betraying one more time his best friend. After he learns aboutThe Woods, Andre starts to put all his effort in trying to find out the truth behind the suicide of Luke and how he became mentally insane. characters.name: Annabeth Moreau
characters.description: Annabeth MoreauActorKeeya KingMaria Nash(Young)GenderFemaleSpeciesSupeHair ColorDark BrownEye ColorBrownAge8("God U.", Flashback)16("First Day")17("H is for Human")OccupationFormer Elementary School StudentHigh School StudentFugitiveMember ofStarlight's ResistanceFamilyMalcolm Moreau- Father †Jackie Moreau- Mother †Marie Moreau- SisterPam Everett- Adoptive MotherEthnicityAfrican-AmericanStatusAliveGen VFirst AppearanceLast Appearance"God U."(Flashback)"Trojan"FateDeath Episode"The Kids Are Not All Right"Annabeth has her throat slit by Cipher and is later resurrected by Marie. Annabeth Moreauis a minor character inSeason Oneand a supporting character inSeason Twoof the Amazon seriesGen V, a spin-off series ofThe Boys. She isMarie Moreau's younger sister. Annabeth was born toMalcolmandJackiein the mid 2000s, and she grew up alongside her older sisterMarie, experiencing an ordinary childhood together. At some point in time in her early life, she was injected withCompound V, subsequently transforming her into aSupeand granting her superhuman powers and abilities In a flashback, Annabeth was shown as a normal happy child playing with her older sister showing a cheerful personality, however when she witnessed her parents dead bodies caused by her sister's power awakening. She developed a deep resentment and during their reunion occasionally snaps at her showing she still blames her for their parents death. She also shows dislike for being a supe and hides the fact she is one from everyone she knows. She's also known to be extremely anxious to the point of it preventing her from going to events, like trick-or-treating, where Marie had to go on her behalf. After talking with jordan and the others annabeth to comes understand her powers more and her sister struggles, After Marie tries to go on suicide mission annabeth reveals that her premonitions foresaw the event that caused their parents death and did nothing to stop it out of fear and anxiety, eventually apologizes to Marie and realizes that she is just a much as to blame for their parents death as she was stating that had she revealed the truth their deaths could have been avoided. After Cipher death Annabeth eventually decides to stay with her sister and friends, the two have finally forgiven each other and start a new relationship and move forward.

characters.name: Ashley Barrett
characters.description: For the Dynamite equivalent of the character, seeJessica Bradley.Ashley BarrettThe BoysGen VActorColby MinifieGenderFemaleSpeciesSupeHuman(Formerly)Hair ColorRedEye ColorBlueAgeLate 20s to Early 30sOccupationFormer Assistant toMadelyn StillwellFormer Director of Talent Relations and Publicist at Vought InternationalFormer Senior Vice President of Hero Management at Vought InternationalFormerDe-jureCEO ofVought InternationalVice President of the United StatesFamily(Unnamed) - Mother †Oh-Father- HusbandEthnicityCaucasian-AmericanStatusAliveThe BoysFirst Appearance"The Name of the Game"Gen VFirst AppearanceLast Appearance"God U.""Guardians of Godolkin" Ashley Barrettis a major antagonist of the Amazon seriesThe Boys. She also appears as a minor antagonist inSeason Oneof its spin-off seriesGen V. She is the current Vice President of the United States of America, under PresidentSteven Calhoun. She formerly served as theDe-jureCEO, Senior Vice President of Hero Management, and Director of Talent Relations forVought International. Like most Vought employees, Ashley is a deceptive self-promoting liar: She works at Vought managing PR duties but rarely displays any kind of real sympathy toward others. Especially with The Seven, she fails to even bother concealing her general disregard for others. She prioritizes promotional events of the famous over any moral and ethical issues and loathes any co-workers who do share her goals. Notable examples include her loudly belittlingStarlightover her public appearance, and ranting about Starlight going off script, completely ignoring the fact thatStarlightsaved a person’s life. Another such example is when she became angry atQueen Maeveafter she broke up withElena. She showed a little bit of compassion and understanding for others was after Maeve was shown to be deeply upset over Elena separating herself from her, due to now knowing the "truth" about her actions for Vought's interest and falling into a self-loathing binge of heterosexual sex to bury her depression, pain and being lectured by Ashley for being "Not Lesbian". Maeve disgusted by her behaviour, tearfully asked her to try and be "a fucking human being for once;" Ashley pauses after realizing how upset she was and showed some remorse for her insensitivity before sincerely apologizing to Maeve about her separation with Elena and gave her space. As shown in the events of season 3, unlike Edgar or Madelyn, Ashley has shown to have begun becoming more outwardly disturbed with Homelander’s more psychotic behaviour and to a degree, has begun becoming more sympathetic to the victims of his actions and even towards the other members of The Seven, such as The Deep, who he has begun terrorizing in order to control. Further evidence of Ashley's kindness and compassion is shown in her behaviour towards Maeve, as seen where she apologized to her after knocking her out with knockout gas in her cell to hide her imprisonment from the authorities and her willingly helping

She is a highly respected scientist atVought Internationalwho conducted experiments on a youngHomelander. Very little is known about Barbara, except that she is a scientist and a high-ranking one working inVought International. As part of a project to create powerfulGod-like Supes,Barbara was assigned to help oversee the process and create one such Supe. She was present the dayHomelanderwas born and lasered his way out of his mother’s womb, describing the gruesome scene like he was a creature from myth or a nightmare, killing three doctors and a nurse, saying, “It went only downhill from there”, implying that the newborn Homelander had killed even more people shortly after his birth. And in the following years, she experimented on a young Homelander, and her role played a crucial part in his development.

Rebecca Butcher(néeSaunders), better known asBecca, is one of the two overarching protagonists (alongsideGrace Mallory) of the Amazon seriesThe Boys. She isBilly Butcher's wife andRyan's mother. After working several years atVought International, she suddenly disappeared on January 24, 2012, leaving her family to believe she was dead and causing Billy to develop a particular hatred towardsHomelanderfor supposedly raping her. Contrary to her husband's vengeful and distrustful personality, Becca is a very compassionate and caring, especially to her son. Throughout Season 2, Becca has done a lot to raise Ryan as a normal child, unlike Homelander, who only wants to train him to use his abilities.

characters.name: Ben's Father
characters.description: Ben's FatherActorNoneGenderMaleHair ColorUnknownAgeUnknownOccupationIndustrial Steel MagnateFamilyBen- Son(Unnamed) - Son †(Unnamed) - Wife †John- GrandsonRyan Butcher- Great-GrandsonEthnicityCaucasian-AmericanStatusDeceasedThe BoysFirst AppearanceLast Appearance"The Instant White-Hot Wild"(Mentioned)"King of Hell"(Mentioned) Thismanis a character mentioned inSeason ThreeandFiveof the Amazon seriesThe Boys. He isSoldier Boy's wealthy father. Ben's father was a prominent industrial magnate based out of South Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, where his son Ben was born in1919. At the height of his power, he owned approximately half of the steel mills in the state of Pennsylvania, making him one of the most financially formidable figures in the region. His wealth stood in direct contrast to the origin story thatVought-Americanwould later construct for his son — the public narrative portrayed Soldier Boy as a poor child from the streets who rose to greatness through hard work, tenacity, and bravery. In reality, Benjamin grew up in a household of considerable privilege, defined not by poverty but by emotional deprivation. His older son was a decorated military veteran who awarded a Medal of Honor for bravery during his service in the Battle of Anzio, whom he considered a war hero to be truly proud of, much to the chagrin and envy of Ben himself. Based onBen's recollections, his father was an exceptionally cold, withholding, and contemptuous man who wielded emotional cruelty with precision. He demonstrated no affection or investment in his son's well-being, and his version of discipline was indifference rather than engagement. He held stringent, rigid notions of masculinity — specifically around the idea of self-sufficiency and earning one's achievements through unassisted effort — which he used as a weapon to diminish his son even at the height of his son's fame. His wealth and social standing appear to have insulated him from any accountability for his failures as a parent.
